description: Anti-pattern guard: sequential per-item processing does not require Loop Over Items. Default per-item iteration already runs items one after another through the chain.

conversation.text: Every 15 minutes, pull pending validation jobs from GET https://api.example.com/jobs/pending — the API returns a JSON envelope {jobs: [...]}, so split that into individual items before processing. Validate each job at POST https://api.example.com/jobs/validate and mark each complete at PATCH https://api.example.com/jobs/{id}/complete.

outcomeExpectations: Each pending job is validated and marked complete via default per-item iteration (fetch → split envelope → validate → complete per job), not via an explicit Loop Over Items / Split In Batches node.
Split Out (or equivalent item expansion from the jobs envelope) is acceptable and expected when the API returns {jobs: [...]} — but the agent did NOT add a Loop Over Items / Split In Batches node.

executionScenarios.name: two-jobs-processed-in-order
executionScenarios.description: Two pending jobs each go through validate then complete
executionScenarios.dataSetup: GET https://api.example.com/jobs/pending returns { "jobs": [{ "id": "job-1" }, { "id": "job-2" }] }. POST https://api.example.com/jobs/validate returns { "ok": true } for each job. PATCH https://api.example.com/jobs/job-1/complete and PATCH https://api.example.com/jobs/job-2/complete each return { "status": "complete" }.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors. Both jobs are validated (POST /jobs/validate called twice) and both are marked complete (PATCH for job-1 and job-2). No errors from miswired loop done/loop outputs.
